revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天富命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Q110064736|qid=Q110064736|1=Ame no Kushimimi}}}}
'''Amatomi no Mikoto''' (天富命) was the {{ill|Grandchild|en|Grandchild|qid=Q3603531|lt=Grandchild|1=Grandchild}} of {{ill|Futodama|en|Futodama|qid=Q11059539|lt=Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of {{ill|Ame-no-Kushimimi-no-Mikoto|ja|天櫛耳命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kushimimi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and father of {{ill|Mimaniki-no-Mikoto|ja|弥麻爾支命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaniki-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. During the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|qid=Q11590444|lt=Emperor Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, he constructed the {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|qid=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Palace|1=Kashihara Shrine}} and later cultivated lands in {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|qid=Q907457|lt=Awa Province|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|ja|房総|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q67433474}}. During Emperor Jimmu's Eastern Expedition, he led the {{ill|Sanuki Inbe|ja|讃岐忌部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Inbe|1=Sanuki Inbe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632550}} and the Kii Inbe, descendants of the two deities {{ill|Taokihooi|ja|タオキホオイ|lt=Taokihooi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=手置帆負命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taokihooi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hikosachi no Mikoto|lt=Hikosachi no Mikoto|lt=Hikosashiri-no-Mikoto|ja|彦狭知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashiri-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, gathered lumber from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, and constructed the palace at the foot of {{ill|Mount Unebi|en|Mount Unebi|qid=Q17606448|lt=Mount Unebi|1=Mount Unebi}} in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 橿原市|1=Kashihara, Nara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q496319}}. He also led various families of the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|qid=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan|1=Inbe clan}} in producing sacred treasures, mirrors, jewels, spears, shields, cotton, and hemp. Among these, the Izumo Tamatsukuri clan, descendants of {{ill|Kushiakarutama-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiakarutama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉祖命|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572729}}, produced prayer beads. Seeking fertile land, he dispatched the Awa Inbe, descendants of {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}, to Awa Province, where they cultivated grains and hemp seeds. This region later became known as {{ill|Oe District, Tokushima|en|Oe District, Tokushima|qid=Q3205811|lt=Oe District|1=Oe District, Tokushima}}. Continuing the search for fertile land, he divided the Awa Inbe and led a group to the {{ill|Eastern Provinces|ja|東国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Provinces|1=Tōgoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10343838}}. There, they sowed hemp and grains, and the land yielding good hemp was called {{ill|Fusa Province|en|Fusa Province|qid=Q2010516|lt=Fusa Province|1=Fusa Province}}; areas where mulberry trees flourished were called {{ill|Yūki District|en|Yūki District|qid=Q1154565|lt=Yūki District|1=Yūki District}}; and the settlement area of the Awa Inbe became known as {{ill|Awa District, Chiba|en|Awa District, Chiba|qid=Q1153597|lt=Awa District|1=Awa District, Chiba}}. Eventually, he built a shrine in honor of his grandfather Futodama-no-Mikoto at this location, which is today's {{ill|Awa Shrine|en|Awa Shrine|qid=Q705547|lt=Awa Shrine|1=Awa Shrine}}, with members of the Inbe clan residing in its shrine households (神戸). The grandchild of Taokihooi-no-Mikoto crafted spear rods, whose descendants later migrated to {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, becoming the Sanuki Inbe clan. Their annual tribute of 800 spear rods alongside taxes signifies this heritage. == References == * ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}'' * ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' – Sections relating to Emperor Jimmu
== See Also == * {{ill|Tomi-no-Misaki Shrine|ja|遠見岬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi-no-Misaki Shrine|1=Tomisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642381}} * {{ill|Mera-zaki Shrine|ja|布良崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mera-zaki Shrine|1=Merasaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19754731}} – Landing site of Amatomi-no-Mikoto * {{ill|Susaki Shrine|en|Susaki Shrine|qid=Q11556513|lt=Sunosaki Shrine|1=Susaki Shrine}} and {{ill|Sunomiya Shrine|ja|洲宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunomiya Shrine|1=Sunomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11556507}} – Shrines dedicated to Amatomi-no-Mikoto’s grandmother Ame-no-Hirume-no-Mikoto {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|天富命|version=103148454|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:天富命]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q24899653}} [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]]
